metadata.title: Why Two Identical Anti-HER2 Drugs Kill Completely Different Tumors
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Cancer Nanomedicine candidate 01. Source: cancer-nanomedicine/chapters/05-antibody-drug-conjugates-as-nanoscale-medicines.md
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = membrane-permeable payload / bystander spread / T-DXd; CRIMSON = killed cells / confined payload / T-DM1 failure in HER2-low; GOLD = key mechanism highlight
metadata.note: EXCLUSIONS: Cut DAR, the five-step dose-loss funnel, linker chemistry details, pneumonitis toxicity, and the DAR-8 failure. Show ONLY: same antibody, one payload stays put, one spreads.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: B01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
B13: palette=claude but the outro is 'OutroCTA' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. A breast cancer patient's tumor tests HER2-low. One drug is approved for it. The other is not. Both drugs carry the exact same HER2-targeting antibody.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: This is Liam, in for Bear. A breast cancer patient's tumor tests HER2-low. One drug is approved for it. The other is not. Both drugs carry the exact same HER2-t

beats.narration_text: T-DM1 and T-DXd. Identical antibodies. One opens a new disease category. One does not. The difference cannot be in the targeting.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: T-DM1 and T-DXd. Identical antibodies. One opens a new disease category. One does not. The difference cannot be in the targeting.

beats.narration_text: Two drugs. Same antibody. One treats HER2-low tumors. The other cannot. The antibody is identical. Why?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two drugs. Same antibody. One treats HER2-low tumors. The other cannot. The antibody is identical. Why?

beats.narration_text: The antibody controls exactly one step: which cell gets bound. In a HER2-low tumor, only a few cells carry the antigen. Most do not.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The antibody controls exactly one step: which cell gets bound. In a HER2-low tumor, only a few cells carry the antigen. Most do not.

beats.narration_text: The reasonable guess: both drugs underperform equally. Antigen is scarce — so little binds, so little kills. Pick whichever tolerates better.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The reasonable guess: both drugs underperform equally. Antigen is scarce — so little binds, so little kills. Pick whichever tolerates better.

beats.narration_text: T-DM1 uses a non-cleavable linker. After the antibody binds and the drug enters the cell, the freed payload carries a charged fragment. Charged means it cannot cross the cell membrane.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: T-DM1 uses a non-cleavable linker. After the antibody binds and the drug enters the cell, the freed payload carries a charged fragment. Charged means it cannot

beats.narration_text: So T-DM1 kills only the cell it entered. The HER2-negative cells around it — the majority in a HER2-low tumor — survive untouched.

shot.image_description: A field of tumor cells rendered as colored circles on a cream newsprint ground. A few bright teal circles represent HER2-positive cells. The majority are muted ink-gray circles representing HER2-negative cells. Inside one teal cell, a small crimson dot represents payload that cannot escape. All surrounding gray cells remain intact. Editorial flat-print style, no gradients, no glow. Camera slowly pushes in on the one cell with the trapped crimson dot.

props.headline: So T-DM1 kills only the cell it entered. The HER2-negative cells…

build.suggested: suggested prompt: So T-DM1 kills only the cell it entered. The HER2-negative cells around it — the majority in a HER2-low tumor — survive untouched.

beats.narration_text: T-DXd uses a cleavable linker, and its freed payload is membrane-permeable. Released inside one HER2-positive cell, it diffuses across the membrane into neighbors.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: T-DXd uses a cleavable linker, and its freed payload is membrane-permeable. Released inside one HER2-positive cell, it diffuses across the membrane into neighbo

beats.narration_text: The antibody is not the whole mechanism. It finds the cell. What happens next depends entirely on whether the payload can cross a membrane.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The antibody is not the whole mechanism. It finds the cell. What happens next depends entirely on whether the payload can cross a membrane.

beats.narration_text: In a HER2-low tumor patch: five HER2-positive cells are all the antibody can reach. T-DM1 kills those five. T-DXd from the same five entry points spreads membrane-permeable payload to roughly forty surrounding cells. Same antibody. Eight times more kills.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: In a HER2-low tumor patch: five HER2-positive cells are all the antibody can reach. T-DM1 kills those five. T-DXd from the same five entry points spreads membra

beats.narration_text: Same antibody. One payload stays put — confined by charge. One payload spreads — carried by membrane permeability. That is why T-DXd, not T-DM1, treats HER2-low breast cancer.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Same antibody. One payload stays put — confined by charge. One payload spreads — carried by membrane permeability. That is why T-DXd, not T-DM1, treats HER2-low
